questions.q: When your institution faces a significant challenge, your first instinct is to:
opts: Call a meeting and gather everyone's input before acting
Diagnose the root cause carefully before taking any action
Take immediate action to resolve the most visible problem
Wait for more information before committing to a direction

questions.q: How clearly can you articulate your institution's vision to a new staff member in 60 seconds?
opts: Very clearly — it's written, shared, and embedded in daily operations
Somewhat clearly — I know it, but it's not consistently communicated
I could explain it, but it varies depending on who you ask
Our vision is more of a feeling than a defined statement

questions.q: If a key member of your team left tomorrow, your core operations would:
opts: Continue smoothly — our processes are documented and shared
Carry on with some disruption while others step in
Struggle in a few critical areas that depend on that person
Be seriously disrupted — too much lives in individual heads

questions.q: When you need to make an important decision without complete information, you:
opts: Use a structured decision framework to evaluate options systematically
Consult my most trusted team members before deciding
Go with my experience — gut instinct is usually right
Delay the decision until I feel more confident

questions.q: Your institution's stated values and how decisions are actually made under pressure are:
opts: Fully aligned — our values guide decisions even when it's hard
Mostly aligned, with occasional gaps
Aligned in principle, but pressure often overrides them
More aspirational than operational

questions.q: How do you handle consistent underperformance in your team?
opts: Structured conversations with clear expectations and a development plan
I address it directly but informally, one-on-one
I give people time and space to improve and hope they do
It's uncomfortable — I tend to work around it

questions.q: Your institution's long-term strategy currently exists as:
opts: A written, phased plan with milestones and accountability structures
A clear direction in my head that I communicate regularly
Broad goals that shift depending on current priorities
We focus on strong year-by-year execution rather than distant planning

questions.q: When it comes to developing the next layer of leaders, you:
opts: Actively build leaders through deliberate delegation and coaching
Mentor a few promising people when time allows
Rely on people to grow into leadership naturally
Handle most key decisions yourself — there isn't time to develop others
